commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A focused repair lane can fix the dispatcher lifecycle wrapper and tests, while maintainer review remains necessary for canonical PR selection and network-boundary acceptance.; Needs attention: No supply-chain issue was found, but the diff intentionally broadens which outbound requests can bypass an operator-configured proxy.; Review finding: [P2] Aggregate bypass dispatcher lifecycle cleanup — src/infra/net/... (sha=7aa4aa6392a9f6a5c4220f111377329c1ea8dc7d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR wraps global EnvHttpProxyAgent construction paths so requests matching OpenClaw's enhanced NO_PROXY matcher use a direct HTTP/1 bypass agent instead of the proxy dispatcher.

PR surface: Source +96, Tests +1. Total +97 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR-introduced lifecycle defect: source and Undici contract inspection show close()/destroy() should expose one completion path, while the wrapper does not wait for bypass cleanup. The original qqbot/COS failure has credible issue logs and terminal real-proxy proof, but I did not run a live qqbot upload in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A focused repair lane can fix the dispatcher lifecycle wrapper and tests, while maintainer review remains necessary for canonical PR selection and network-boundary acceptance.

target.title: fix(proxy): apply enhanced NO_PROXY matching to global undici dispatcher (fixes #95998)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #95998.

When `HTTPS_PROXY` is set, the global undici `EnvHttpProxyAgent` routes ALL requests through the proxy — including internal plugin requests that should bypass via `NO_PROXY`. The built-in undici NO_PROXY matcher does not support leading-dot subdomain patterns, CIDR notation, or octet wildcards.

OpenClaw already has an enhanced `matchesNoProxy()` that handles these — but it was only used by provider HTTP helpers, not the global dispatcher.

## Changes Made

1. **NO_PROXY-aware dispatcher wrapper**: Wraps the global EnvHttpProxyAgent so each dispatch checks OpenClaw`s enhanced `matchesNoProxy()` before routing.
2. **All construction paths wrapped**: Applied in all three places that build an EnvHttpProxyAgent.
3. **Timeout/connect policy preserved**: The direct bypass agent now inherits the same timeout and connect options as the EnvHttpProxyAgent.

## Evidence

- **Unit tests:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/net/proxy-env.test.ts` — 55/55 passed
- **Dispatcher tests:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/net/undici-global-dispatcher.test.ts` — 33/33 passed

## Real behavior proof

Real proxy server started on 127.0.0.1, HTTPS_PROXY configured, tested bypass vs non-bypass:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/net/no-proxy-full-proof.test.ts

1. ✅ COS .myqcloud.com → bypass proxy (matchesNoProxy=true)
2. ✅ OpenAI API → use proxy (matchesNoProxy=false)
3. ✅ localhost → bypass proxy (matchesNoProxy=true)
4. ✅ CIDR 100.64.0.0/10 → bypass proxy (matchesNoProxy=true)

Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  1 passed (1)
```

- [x] AI-assisted
target.author: zw-xysk

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
